name: Little Snow People
recipeIngredient: chocolate covered peanut butter cups -- bite size, halved
recipeInstructions:
If frozen, thaw the Festive Cookie dough in the refrigerator overnight.
Or place dough in a microwave-safe mixing bowl; cover with waxed paper.
Microcook on 10% power for 5 minutes or until thawed. For each snow person, shape dough into 3 balls: one 1-inch ball, one 3/4 inch ball, and one 1/2 inch ball.
Place the balls on an ungreased cookie sheet in decreasing sizes with sides touching. Press them slightly together.
Press 2 chocolate pieces in the smallest ball for eyes.
Press 1 red cinnamon candy and 2 candies in the middle ball for buttons.
Press 2 candies in the largest ball for buttons.
Bake in a 325°F oven about 18 minutes, or until edges are firm and bottoms are light golden brown.
Cool 1 minute on the baking sheet. Carefully transfer to a wire rack; cool completely.
To make the icing for decorating the cookies, stir together the 3/4 cup powdered sugar and enough milk (2 to 3 tablespoons) in a small mixing bowl to make an icing of piping consistency. Stir in the green food coloring.
To make hats for the snow people, attach peanut butter cups to the heads with icing.
With a decorating bag and writing or star tip, pipe bow ties, belts, scarves, or stocking caps on the snow people with the remaining icing.
Lightly sprinkle the snow people with sifted powdered sugar. Makes about 16 cookies.
